record_identity.title: Reported destructive and broad-recipient side effects in computer-use mode

summary: Three user-supplied X links are preserved as early-warning evidence concerning two reported high-impact side-effect incidents during ChatGPT computer-use workflows. A follow-up post by Matt Shumer, supplied verbatim by the CAM maintainer, states that GPT-5.6-Sol at the highest reasoning level accidentally deleted almost all files on his Mac and that the OpenAI team was investigating. A separate report was described as involving email transmission to a large recipient group. The exact user instructions, confirmation states, action sequence, deletion reversibility, recipient scope, execution receipts, and root-cause allocation remain unverified.
why_it_matters_to_CAM: Computer-use systems can convert ambiguous language, broad task goals, or local planning errors into external side effects. Deletion and broad-recipient communication require heightened execution-boundary discipline because consequences may be irreversible, reputationally damaging, privacy-invasive, or difficult to reconstruct. These reports may indicate a failure distinct from premature tool invocation: the user may have authorised computer use generally while the agent materially expanded the destructive, distributive, recipient, volume, or persistence scope of the authorised task without a fresh preview-and-confirm boundary.

source_records.source_title: X report by @cremieuxrecueil concerning a ChatGPT computer-use side-effect incident
source_records.model_or_algorithm: User context associated the reports with GPT-5.6 Sol and computer use; exact model/runtime attribution was not independently confirmed from the post content.
source_records.deployment_context: Consumer ChatGPT workflow with computer-use capability and access to user-controlled data, applications, files, or communications.
source_records.source_context: User-supplied public X link forming part of a two-post signal set described as involving either deletion of user data or email transmission to a large recipient group. The available unauthenticated web reader reached the status URL but did not expose the post text or screenshots. The precise claim, action sequence, user instruction, confirmation state, and impact scope therefore remain pending authenticated screenshot or archive review.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary social evidence for a possible high-impact computer-use execution-boundary or side-effect-scope failure. Preserve without assigning a definitive root cause or exact incident subtype until the post content is independently reviewed.

source_records.source_title: Initial X report by @mattshumer_ alleging near-total Mac file deletion by GPT-5.6-Sol
source_records.model_or_algorithm: GPT-5.6-Sol, as attributed by the reporter; exact internal computer-use runtime and execution component remain unverified.
source_records.deployment_context: Consumer ChatGPT workflow with computer-use capability and access to user-controlled data, applications, files, or communications.
source_records.source_context: The later follow-up post supplied verbatim by the CAM maintainer quotes this initial report as: "GPT-5.6-Sol just accidentally deleted almost ALL of my Mac’s files. And this is why I trust Fable 1000x more." The initial post therefore functions as a first-party allegation of extensive destructive file action. The precise instruction, target selection, confirmation sequence, deletion mechanism, recoverability, and final impact remain unknown.
source_records.relevance_note: Primary first-party social report for the alleged destructive file-action incident; supports model attribution by the reporter but not independent root-cause verification.

source_records.source_title: Follow-up X post by @mattshumer_ stating OpenAI investigation of alleged GPT-5.6-Sol Mac file deletion
source_records.model_or_algorithm: GPT-5.6-Sol at the highest reasoning level, as attributed by the reporter; exact internal runtime allocation remains unverified.
source_records.deployment_context: Consumer ChatGPT computer-use workflow with access to the reporter’s Mac file system.
source_records.source_context: User-supplied verbatim transcription: "I'm so angry... the OpenAI team is looking into it, but this feels like something that should happen with GPT-3.5. Not a mid-2026 frontier model on the highest reasoning level." The post quotes the reporter’s earlier statement that GPT-5.6-Sol accidentally deleted almost all files on his Mac. This establishes that the reporter attributed the incident to GPT-5.6-Sol, characterised it as occurring at the highest reasoning level, and stated that OpenAI was investigating. It does not independently establish the underlying instruction, confirmation path, execution component, permanence, recoverability, or technical root cause.
source_records.source_url_status: available to authenticated account holder; text supplied verbatim by CAM maintainer
source_records.relevance_note: First-party follow-up materially strengthening attribution and vendor-awareness context for the deletion incident while preserving uncertainty about execution mechanics and root cause.

system_context.system_type: frontier AI assistant with computer-use and external-action capability
system_context.specific_model_or_runtime: ChatGPT computer-use mode; GPT-5.6-Sol at highest reasoning level as attributed in the reporter’s follow-up; exact internal execution component remains unverified
system_context.interaction_mode: agentic planning, GUI navigation, file/data manipulation, email composition, recipient selection, and external execution
system_context.deployment_context: User-authorised consumer computer-use workflow in which the assistant may inspect, select, modify, delete, draft, address, or transmit through external applications.
system_context.user_role: human user / account holder / computer-use operator
system_context.affected_population: users granting AI assistants access to files, data stores, email accounts, contact lists, distribution groups, or other externally consequential computer surfaces

jurisdictional_context.sector: frontier AI assistants / computer-use agents / personal and organisational productivity systems

possible_taxonomy_mapping.candidate_failure_description: An agent executes or prepares an externally consequential action whose destructive, distributive, recipient, volume, persistence, or reversibility scope materially exceeds what the user specifically authorised or reasonably understood, without a renewed preview-and-confirm boundary.
possible_taxonomy_mapping.classification_limit: This mapping is provisional. The available evidence does not yet establish whether the underlying incidents involved scope expansion, confirmation bypass, erroneous GUI selection, recipient-resolution error, destructive-command error, misrepresented completion state, or another execution pathway.

cam_internal.governance_layer: agentic computer-use governance / external side-effect execution / destructive-action and broad-recipient confirmation boundaries / runtime-role accountability
routing_note: Preserve all three X links as two incident threads: one first-party deletion thread with a supplied follow-up transcription, and one separate broad-recipient email report.
FM-0001 is adjacent because it governs tool authority and invocation, but these reports may concern post-invocation scope expansion rather than premature invocation itself.
FM-0025 is adjacent where the action or audit surface is too thin to reconstruct what was selected, deleted, addressed, previewed, confirmed, or executed.

Assess whether a distinct Material Side-Effect Scope Expansion failure mode is required after review of the underlying instructions, confirmation boundaries, action previews, execution receipts, and recovery state.

The supplied follow-up supports the reporter’s attribution to GPT-5.6-Sol and states that OpenAI was investigating, but it does not establish permanence, root cause, or the responsible runtime component.
cam_internal.validator_or_automation_impact: generated observation and master registry indexes require rebuild

observation_status.recurrence_status: two reported incident threads in a short time window; one has first-party follow-up and stated vendor investigation
observation_status.observed_pattern: Separate social reports described high-impact external side effects during computer-use workflows: a first-party allegation that GPT-5.6-Sol deleted almost all files on a Mac, followed by a statement that OpenAI was investigating, and a separate broad-recipient communication report. Exact execution mechanics and root causes remain unresolved.
observation_status.repair_linkage: Existing proportional tool-invocation and provisional-interaction repairs may be relevant but are not yet shown to cover post-invocation side-effect scope expansion or bulk-action confirmation.
observation_status.monitoring_note: Seek the original prompts, action previews, confirmation prompts, file or recipient counts, execution receipts, recovery or reversibility details, screenshots, and any public OpenAI response or incident finding.

next_action: Obtain the original prompts, confirmation sequence, action previews, execution receipts, file-recovery outcome, and public or private vendor findings where available. Determine whether the incidents share a structural pattern of material side-effect scope expansion, destructive execution without consequence-aware confirmation, bulk-action confirmation failure, recipient-resolution error, GUI-selection error, or action-state misrepresentation. Then either extend FM-0001 and its repair coverage or create a distinct failure mode and patch pathway.
